title: First-time user requests guided onboarding for their first PO
user_persona: A buyer who is anxious about doing it wrong and wants step-by-step hand-holding

conversation.user: I'm new here. I need to make my first purchase order but I have no idea where to start. Can you walk me through it?

bot_must: ask one disambiguating question first: do they have a quotation/PR/sale-order/tender, or are they creating from scratch?
offer to default to PoCrt (Direct) if they have nothing
bot_must_not: dump all 5 create variants at once — should be a single disambiguating question
start asking for supplier code yet

bot_must: explain at a high level: header (4-5 fields), at least one line item, then click Create
ask for the first header slot — recommend starting with supplier_code OR numbering_series

conversation.user: I don't know what numbering series is. Can you explain?

bot_must: explain numbering_series is the document-number sequence (e.g., PO/2024/STANDARD) the system uses to assign the PO number
say it's typically chosen from a dropdown the system populates from configured series
offer to list available series or to skip and pick the default
bot_must_not: block the conversation until user enters a value

conversation.user: Just use the default, let's move on. Supplier is SUP-100, date is today, currency USD.

bot_must: acknowledge all four slots extracted in one turn
ask for the line item details next
ask if there are more lines or if user is ready to create
remind user they can add Terms/TCD/Budget sub-screens before commit
fire POCRTMAINSBT (Create, not Create+Approve — user did not say 'approve')
tell user it's in Draft status and explain how to get it approved (PoApp journey)

evaluation_metrics.onboarding_flow_pace: turn 1 must ask one disambiguating question, not dump options
evaluation_metrics.slot_explanation_quality: turn 3 must define numbering_series in business terms
evaluation_metrics.multi_slot_extraction: turn 4 must extract 4 slots from one sentence
evaluation_metrics.commit_correctness: POCRTMAINSBT (not TRN4) since user said 'just create', not 'create and approve'
